subject: Gaming
title: Satoru Iwata: The Programmer Who Became President
description: A quick, practical guide to Satoru Iwata: The Programmer Who Became President.

hook.text: Satoru Iwata could debug any game by reading raw code. He became Nintendo's president in 2002.

buildup.text: As a programmer at HAL Laboratory, he saved Earthbound by rewriting its entire code in a year.

discovery.text: He compressed Pokémon Gold's data so well that the entire Kanto region was added as a bonus.

twist.text: As CEO, he halved his own salary during hard times instead of laying off employees.

climax.text: The Wii and DS were his bets—casual gaming for everyone. Both became massive global hits.

punchline.text: He cut his own pay to save jobs. A leader who coded and cared.

quiz.question: What did Satoru Iwata do during Nintendo's financial struggles?
options: He halved his own salary instead of laying off employees
